heroDesc: Select materials by strength, weight, corrosion resistance, conductivity, insulation, finishing needs and manufacturing route.
sectionDesc: Common materials are organized around lightweight structures, corrosion resistance, conductivity, high-load parts and insulating wear needs, so material, process, finishing and cost choices can be evaluated together early.

cards.title: Aluminum Alloys
cards.body: Low density and stable machinability for housings, brackets, fixtures and lightweight structures.

cards.title: Stainless Steel
cards.body: Good corrosion resistance and durability for equipment parts, shafts, fluid-contact and clean environments.

cards.title: Carbon Steel
cards.body: Balanced strength and cost for load structures, bases, shafts and mechanical connectors.

cards.title: Copper Alloys
cards.grades: Pure copper, H62, C36000, tin bronze
cards.body: Strong electrical and thermal conductivity for contacts, heat-transfer parts, bushings and wear pairs.

cards.title: Engineering Plastics
cards.body: Lightweight, insulating, wear-resistant or chemical-resistant options for prototypes and lower-load structures.

cards.title: Titanium Alloys
cards.body: High specific strength and corrosion resistance for demanding lightweight structures and special environments.

cards.title: Composites
cards.grades: Carbon fiber sheet, fiberglass sheet, G10/FR4
cards.body: Suitable for lightweight, insulating, laminated structures and directional stiffness needs.

faq.title: Materials FAQ
faq.desc: These answers help clarify fit, input files and quotation review before submitting a manufacturing request.

items.q: What projects is Materials suitable for?
items.a: Materials is suitable for projects that need manufacturing review based on drawings, material, quantity, tolerances and delivery goals, especially prototypes, low-volume parts and functional components.

items.q: What information is needed for a Materials quote?
items.a: Prepare 3D files, 2D drawings when tolerances matter, material grade, quantity, finish requirements, inspection needs and target delivery timing so engineers can evaluate the route accurately.

items.q: How are lead time and cost confirmed for Materials?
items.a: Lead time and cost are confirmed after reviewing geometry, material, process route, finishing, inspection scope and order quantity. Complex tolerances or special finishes may require engineering confirmation.

ctaTitle: Need material substitution or selection advice?
ctaDesc: Share the part use, environment, load requirements and target cost so the material can be reviewed together with process and finishing needs.
